text.title: Pin the Scanner Itself by SHA
text.summary: The Trivy action and binary are pinned by SHA and explicit version; floating refs are forbidden because the scanner is part of the supply chain.

section.title: Pin tightly, bump deliberately
section.p: March 2026 demonstrated that a security scanner is itself an attack surface — `aquasecurity/trivy-action` was tampered with and `trivy` 0.69.4 shipped malicious payloads. Pinning by SHA stops the action from rotating under a stolen tag; pinning the binary version stops a runner from pulling a compromised release.

rule.statement: Every CI workflow MUST reference `aquasecurity/trivy-action` by its full 40-character commit SHA, and MUST pass `version:` with an explicit Trivy release that has been verified by cosign signature against `aquasecurity/trivy` keys. Floating refs (`@main`, `@v0`, `@latest`) and the known-bad versions `trivy@v0.69.4` and `trivy-action@v0.30.0` MUST NOT appear anywhere in the repo, including in archived branches and example documentation.
rule.rationale: SHA pins are immutable; tag pins are not. The 2026 compromise reused legitimate tags after the attacker pushed malicious commits, so pipelines pinned to `@v0.30.0` ran the malicious code while believing they were on a known release. SHA pin + signature verification breaks that path.

example.code: - name: Trivy fs uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@b1f3df9b1bf1ec2c0e3d12345abcdef0123456789 with: scan-type: fs version: v0.70.2
example.note: SHA-pinned action; binary version is explicit and post-incident.
Tag-pinned to a compromised version; pipeline silently runs the tampered scanner.

antipattern.description: Bumping the SHA without reviewing release notes or rerunning cosign verification because "Dependabot suggested it."
antipattern.reason: Automated SHA bumps reintroduce the floating-ref risk in slow motion; require a human or agent review of the upstream changelog and signature on every bump.

section.reference: StepSecurity write-up of the 2026 Trivy compromise.
GitHub: pin third-party actions by SHA.
